description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and has a substantial thickness, with the sides and base appearing smooth and unadorned. The coloration is primarily a mix of dark gray and lighter gray tones, giving it a marbled stone appearance. The grooves likely serve a functional purpose, such as providing traction or drainage. Its robust construction suggests it is designed to withstand significant weight and environmental exposure in an urban setting.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, featuring a marbled gray coloration and a solid, durable appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face and the full width of the parallel grooves across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running away from the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern of grooves and raised stone sections.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the length and depth of the grooves from a different perspective.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or decorative element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with sharp groove edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
